_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to develop host-based IDS/IPS signatures and settings
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing and implementing data anonymization and pseudonymization techniques appropriate to the intended use, including k-anonymity, differential privacy, and synthetic data generation approaches.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-S-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.380), SP-T-010 (SP, cosine: 0.377).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.8/10 (raw 0.377). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [ability, based, develop, host, ids, ips] vs WIDAI-only terms [anonymity, anonymization, approaches, appropriate, data, designing] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.854 (P=0.862, R=0.846). Balanced P/R — the 8-word DCWF element and 24-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Ability to develop host-based IDS/IPS signatures and settings" and "Skill in designing and implementing data anonymization and pseudonymization tech" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.382 ≈ STS 0.377 (gap 0.005). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [none] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 6.7%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [ability, based, develop, host, ids, ips]. WIDAI terms: [anonymity, anonymization, approaches, appropriate, data, designing].
